perturbation.original_text: 8. Equitable Relief. Discloser and Recipient agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate Discloser’s damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein , especially with regard any breach associat ed with Confidential Information that was to be maintained behind the Information Barrier . Accordingly, the Recipient agrees that in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, Discloser will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. Recipient further agrees that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and that Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. If any action or proceeding is brought to enforce this Agreement because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, should it prevail, Discloser shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in suc h action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Equitable Relief. Discloser and Recipient agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate Discloser’s damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein , especially with regard any breach associat ed with Confidential Information that was to be maintained behind the Information Barrier . Accordingly, the Recipient agrees that in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, Discloser will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. Recipient further agrees that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and that Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. Recipient shall promptly return all tangible information, including any and all c opies or partial copies thereof and thereupon confirm destruction of all information held electronically. If any action or proceeding is brought to enforce this Agreement because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, should it prevail, Discloser shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in suc h action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the return of tangible information clause to the equitable relief section obscures the distinct obligation to return confidential information upon request or termination of the agreement, mixing it with remedies for breach. This makes it harder to enforce as a separate contractual duty.
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Business and Commerce Code Section 134A.004 addresses remedies for misappropriation of trade secrets, including injunctive relief. By placing the return of information clause within the section discussing equitable relief, it implies that the return is only relevant in cases of misappropriation, not as a general obligation upon termination of the agreement. This could limit the discloser's ability to enforce the return of confidential information outside of a misappropriation claim.
perturbation.justification: The original contract maintains a clear structure where obligations and remedies are distinct. The changed text, by embedding the return of information clause within the equitable relief section, obscures its nature as a separate obligation triggered not only by a breach but also upon termination, creating a structural flaw. While no scraped law snippets are available, the Perturbation and Law Explanations highlight that this obfuscation could limit the discloser's ability to enforce the return of confidential information outside of a misappropriation claim. This shift makes the enforcement of the return of information more difficult, creating a legal contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Term, Non -Disclosure and Use. For a period of two (2) years from the dat e of receipt, all Confidential I nformation shall be maintained in confidence by Recipient , shall not be disclosed , published, communicate d, divulged or revealed in any manner, to any other person, firm, corporation or other third party entity, including employees of Recipient who do not have a need to know , or who should not know any such details pertaining to Confidential Information in the course of their employment , except as may otherwise provided in this Agreement. Recipient further affirmatively agrees not to use any Confidential I nformation received from Discloser except for the express purposes set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. General Provisions. The following general provisions shall apply: Recipient further affirmatively agrees not to use any Confidential I nformation received from Discloser except for the express purposes set forth herein. (a) Governing Law: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Texas , without regard to its conflict of laws provisions . (b) Severability: If one or more of the provision s in this Agreement are deemed void by law, then the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. (c) Successors and Assigns: This Agreement will be binding upon the successors and/or assigns of the parties hereto . (d) Headings. All headi ngs used herein are intended for reference purposes only and shall not affect the interpretation, or validity of this Agreement. (e) Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be in writing and executed by a duly authoriz ed representative of either the Discloser or Recipient .
perturbation.explanation: I moved the Recipient's agreement not to use confidential information for any purpose other than those stated into the General Provisions. This section mostly contains boilerplate legal terms, making it less noticeable. This makes it harder for the Discloser to point to this clause as a central obligation, since the clause is about usage, but located under General Provisions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Business and Commerce Code Section 134A.002 defines Misappropriation and makes unlawful the use of trade secrets. By burying the use restriction within general provisions, the contract obscures the core restriction against using the confidential information for unapproved purposes, undermining the enforceability of this provision related to trade secrets.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation type is 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction'. The original contract had a specific clause about maintaining confidentiality. The changed text moved this clause into 'General Provisions', making it less prominent. The Law Explanation states that this undermines the enforceability of the provision related to trade secrets by obscuring the core restriction. The moved clause relates to using confidential information received from the Discloser, but is located under 'General Provisions', making it a structural flaw that contradicts the clarity of the agreement.
